UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS BANK OF AMERICA, RECONTRUST COMPANY, N.A. AND HSBC BANK USA, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 30 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. SUSTAINED AS TO 2ND, 3RD, 4TH & 6TH CAUSE OF ACTIONS, WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO INCLUDE ALLEGATION THAT DIABLO FUNDING GROUP DID NOT ASSIGN ITS INTEREST IN THE MORTGAGE PRIOR TO ITS SUSPENSION. AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST ALSO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING INJURY IN FACT IN THE FORM OF LOST MONEY OR PROPERTY. AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST ALSO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING DEFENDANTS OWED HER A DUTY OF CARE BECAUSE DEFENDANTS OPERATED AS MORE THAN SIMPLY LENDERS. SEE NYMARK V. HEART FEDERAL (1991) 231 CAL.APP.3D 1089, 1096. SUSTAINED AS TO 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. SUSTAINED AS TO 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Jason Estavillo, 510-982-3001 for Plaintiff/Opposing party Josey Russell.
Joel Spann, 415-398-3344 for Defendants/Moving parties Recontrust, NA, Bank of America, NA and HSBC Bank USA, NA.

Argued by Joel Spann counsel for the defendants. Jason Estavillo addresses Mr. Spann's arguement and submits the the tentative ruling. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT RECONTRUST COMPANY, N.A., BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., HSBC BANK USA, N.A., AS TRUSTEE FOR THE HOLDERS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication

DENIED.
I. As to issue 1 (1st cause of action) moving party failed to shift its burden and
(1) Failed to show that Plaintiff was in default on her loan, i.e.
(a) that her minimum payments changed from the original payment amount prior to December 2011 (Note paragraph 1, 2(A), 3(B)(i) and (ii), 3(C) and Disclosure Statement). The evidence relied upon for this proposition (Motion 6: 9-11; SS, Issue 1, facts 4 and 7) shows that the change was possible and does not show that the change in fact occurred; or
(b) that the escrow amounts have been demanded to be repaid per the terms of the DOT, paragraph 9 ("These amounts shall bear interest at the Note rate from the date of disbursement and shall be payable, with such interest, upon notice from Lender to Borrower requesting payment" ); The evidence relied upon for this proposition (Motion 6:12 "and sought repayment from Russell" and SS issue 1, facts 6 and 13) shows that the demand could have been made, but does not show that it was in fact made.
(2) Failed to show that the beneficial interest in the Note and Deed of Trust was "transferred" to Countrywide Financial. Even if the Bailee Letter, which is not purported to be originated or received by Bank of America from the sender in its regular course of business, were admissible evidence, it does not show that Countrywide Financial ever "accepted" it or ever paid the purchased price. Additionally, no evidence is presented to show a connection between Countrywide Financial and Bank of America N.A., as successor by merger to BAC Home Loans Servicing LP FKA Countrywide Home Loans Servicing LP

II. As to issue 2 (3rd cause of action) moving party failed to shift its burden and "establish that the loan was transferred in May 2007 (Motion 7:22-23; SS Issue 2, fact 3). See I(2) above.
III. As to issue 3 (5th cause of action) moving party fails to shift its burned and show that Plaintiff's minimum payment amount not only could have changed under the contract, but in fact did change (Motion 8:11-17; Issue 3, facts 6, 7, 8, 10, 11) in a way other than indicated in the Note paragraphs 1, 2(A), 3(B)(i) and (ii), 3(C) and Disclosure Statement; or that Plaintiff was notified of the changes per paragraph 4 of the Note; and/or that payment of escrow amount was requested per paragraph 9 of the DOT.
IV. As to issues 4 and 5 (2nd cause of action) Defendants argue that summary adjudication is proper because the preceding causes of action are wholly without merit "according [sic], this claim must likewise fail" (Motion 9:3-5) and because Plaintiff failed to submit her contractually required payments (Motion 10:1-2). Since Defendants failed to establish that the other causes of action fail, the Motion for Summary Adjudication as to this cause of action is DENIED. Finally, to the extent Defendants argue that UCL cause of action fails because Plaintiff has not pleaded restitution damages (Motion 10:6), Plaintiff is seeking injunction (Third Amended Complaint 25: 18-21), a remedy available for a violation of B&P 17200.
Therefore Motion for Summary Adjudication as to issues 1-5 is DENIED and Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk: J. Murphy; Reported: Carol Haraburda, CSR#8052, 925-360-6065. (501)

Jason Estavillo, Esq., Attorney at Law, (510-982-3001), for Joey Russell, Plaintiff and opposing party.
